era_context: The Early Iron Age is well-established in the Mediterranean and Near East. Egypt under Ramesses XI is in its final decades. Assyria continues a period of modest expansion under Ashur-bel-kala or his successors. The Levant is dominated by emerging city-states and maritime powers. Greece is in the Geometric period. Iron-working technology is becoming standard in many regions, though bronze remains important for prestige items and weapons.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of Mesopotamia and the Levant interior

events.title: Ramesses XI's reign in its final phase
events.description: Ramesses XI, the last pharaoh of the Twentieth Dynasty, is in the final years of his reign (which ended c. 1069 BCE by conventional chronology). Egypt is effectively divided between the pharaonic authority in Lower Egypt and the High Priests of Amun in Upper Egypt. The transition to the Third Intermediate Period is imminent. The pharaoh's authority is nominal in much of the country.
events.certainty_note: Ramesses XI's reign is well-attested; the specific events of this year are not individually documented.

disconfirming_evidence: The dating of Ramesses XI's reign is debated. Some scholars propose a longer reign (c. 1099–1069 BCE), while others suggest a shorter one. The notion of a 'division' of Egypt may overstate the degree of political separation—the pharaonic system persisted, albeit weakened.
historiographic_note: Documentation for this year is minimal. Sources are primarily Egyptian administrative records. Events outside Egypt and outside the sphere of state activity are almost entirely absent from the surviving record.

graph_edges.note: Ramesses XI's reign ends; Third Intermediate Period begins
